title: PYDANTIC-0003 -- a deeply nested core-schema overflows the native stack at SchemaValidator build (hard crash, uncatchable)
panic_reason: stack overflow (not a Rust panic) in src/validators/mod.rs build_validator_inner -- recursion with no depth guard at construction
one_line_repro: from pydantic_core import SchemaValidator, core_schema as cs; s=cs.int_schema(); [s:=cs.list_schema(s) for _ in range(5000)]; SchemaValidator(s)
expected: a too-deeply-nested schema should raise (e.g. a recursion/SchemaError) at build; it must not overflow the native stack and crash the process
reliability: n=1000 builds fine; n>=~3000 crashes (threshold depends on stack size / build). HARD crash: panic=unwind cannot catch a stack overflow. Confirmed via AddressSanitizer 'stack-overflow' at build_validator_inner.
target_python: CPython 3.14t free-threaded + ASan (stack-overflow caught by ASan); a plain build SIGSEGVs
found_in: design-phase probing while building fusil_pydantic_plugin; reproduced by the plugin's seed_deep_nesting factory
prior_art: UNREPORTED (tracker checked 2026-07-21). pydantic-core DOES guard recursion at validate/serialize time (RecursionState / _recursion_limit -> clean recursion_loop error) but NOT at schema CONSTRUCTION -- the RecursionState guard is instantiated only at validate/serialize (src/validators/mod.rs:354/381/433), never during build. So build_validator_inner recurses per level with no counter.
notes: build_validator_inner (src/validators/mod.rs, ~:551 dispatch) recurses on each nested child schema (list/dict/union/nullable/chain/default/... item schemas) with no depth guard, so a schema nested a few thousand levels deep overflows the native stack during SchemaValidator(...)/SchemaSerializer(...). Distinct from cyclic definition-ref schemas, which ARE guarded (RecursionError at validate). Fix = add a construction-time depth guard (mirror the validate-time RecursionState, or a build depth counter) that returns SchemaError past a limit. DoS class: the only hard (uncatchable) crash of the pydantic-core set.
